component.name: IconButton
component.description: A square, icon-only button that triggers an action with no text label; the compact sibling of Button for toolbars, rows, and adornments.
component.note: Square (radius.none); default renders a 20px icon, small a 16px icon. Shares Button's appearance tokens and selected/disabled treatment, but is icon-only so aria-label is required. Synced to Figma '❖ Icon Button'.

spacing: spacing.none (no inner padding — icon centered in a square box)

useCases: Compact actions in a toolbar where text labels would crowd the bar (neutral)
Inline row controls in a table — edit, copy, more (small, text or neutral)
Adornment inside an input — clear, reveal password, search trigger (small)
Persistent toggles like bold/italic or an active filter (isSelected, aria-pressed)
Destructive affordance such as a delete/remove icon (danger)
A single icon-only primary moment, e.g. an add/compose action (primary)

commonPatterns.name: Toolbar of icon buttons
commonPatterns.description: A row of neutral icon buttons for formatting or view controls; each carries its own aria-label.

commonPatterns.name: Inline row action
commonPatterns.description: A small icon button inside a table row or input adornment — minimal weight until hover.

commonPatterns.name: Active toggle
commonPatterns.description: isSelected renders the forest tint and exposes aria-pressed; pair with onClick to flip state.

commonPatterns.name: Destructive delete affordance
commonPatterns.description: A danger icon button for removing an item; gate irreversible deletes behind a confirmation.

commonPatterns.name: Icon-only compose action
commonPatterns.description: A single primary icon button as the lone forest.500 moment, e.g. a floating add control.

antiPatterns.reason: There is no visible text, so screen-reader users hear nothing — the action is unannounced and unusable.
antiPatterns.alternative: Always pass a clear, action-oriented aria-label (e.g. 'Delete article').

antiPatterns.scenario: Using an IconButton where the action would be clearer with a label
antiPatterns.reason: An ambiguous glyph forces users to guess; an icon-only control should only be used when the meaning is obvious.
antiPatterns.alternative: Use a Button with a text label (optionally with iconBefore) when the icon alone is unclear.

antiPatterns.scenario: Two primary icon buttons on the same view (or a primary IconButton competing with a Button primary)
antiPatterns.reason: Breaks the one forest.500 moment per view rule — they compete for the primary action.
antiPatterns.alternative: Keep one primary across the whole view; demote the rest to neutral or text.

antiPatterns.scenario: Wrapping a bare clickable <Icon> in an onClick instead of using IconButton
antiPatterns.reason: A clickable Icon has no button semantics, no focus ring, no keyboard activation, and no accessible name.
antiPatterns.alternative: Use IconButton, which renders a real <button> with role, focus, keyboard support, and aria-label.

slots.icon: Required — the glyph to render, by IconName; sized to 20px (default) or 16px (small)

interactions.hover: primary → forest.700 fill; neutral/text → forest.100 tint + forest.500 border + forest.700 icon; danger → danger.800
interactions.press: primary → forest.900; neutral/text → forest.200 tint; danger → danger.900
interactions.selected: forest.50 fill + forest.700 border + forest.700 icon across all appearances; sets aria-pressed
interactions.disabled: neutral.100 fill (transparent for text), neutral.400 icon, transparent border, cursor not-allowed

accessibility.keyboardSupport: Enter and Space activate; Tab moves focus in/out
accessibility.screenReader: Announces the required aria-label; isSelected announces 'pressed' via aria-pressed. The inner Icon is aria-hidden so only the label is read.
accessibility.contrastNote: Danger fill uses danger.700 (white icon 5.81:1) rather than Figma's danger.500 (3.54:1, fails AA), matching Button.

figmaDivergences.reason: Figma's danger.500 + white icon is 3.54:1, failing WCAG AA. danger.700 is 5.81:1. Same AA-first approach as Button.

aiHints.context: Reach for IconButton when an action is best represented by a single, unambiguous glyph and space is tight — toolbars, table rows, input adornments, and toggles. Always supply aria-label. Default to neutral; use text for the lowest weight, danger for destructive icons, isSelected (aria-pressed) for persistent toggles. Reserve primary for the single forest.500 moment on the view.
neverUseWhen: You cannot give a clear aria-label — an unlabeled icon button is unusable
The icon's meaning is not obvious — use a Button with a text label instead
The surface already has a primary action — use neutral or text, never a second primary
You only need a static or clickable glyph — never wrap a bare <Icon>; use IconButton for real button semantics
